dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8961 IH: Student Suicide Prevention Awareness Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Carter of Louisiana (for himself and Mr. Moylan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title V of the Public Health Service Act to provide for a public awareness campaign for schools regarding youth suicide prevention resources, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Student Suicide Prevention Awareness Act of 2026.

section.header: Public awareness campaign for schools regarding youth suicide prevention resources
section.text: Section 520B of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 290bb–32) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking and at the end;
by redesignating paragraph (3) as paragraph (4); and
by inserting after paragraph (2) the following:

paragraph.text: carrying out, in coordination with the Secretary, a public awareness campaign on youth suicide prevention resources, including the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, that—

subparagraph.text: includes public outreach with respect to the development of such campaign; and
provides support to schools to update signage relating to the availability of such resources that meets the requirements specified in subsection (d); and

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (d) through (g) as subsections (e) through (h), respectively;
by inserting after subsection (c) the following:

subsection.text: The requirements specified in this subsection with respect to signage relating to the availability of youth suicide prevention resources are that the signage be clear and visible signage that—

subparagraph.text: the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline phone number;
information that students can access support by—

clause.text: calling or sending a text message to such phone number; or
using digital chat services provided by the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline;

subparagraph.text: language encouraging students experiencing emotional distress to seek help; and
language indicating that the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is free, confidential, and available 24 hours per day and 7 days per week; and

paragraph.text: is displayed, or digitally posted, as applicable, in locations easily visible to students, including—

subparagraph.text: entrances and exits of the school;
student common areas;
school counselor offices;
school nurse offices;
gymnasiums;
auditoriums;
art rooms;
music rooms;
libraries;
student restrooms (including bathroom stalls and sinks) and locker rooms; and
digital school bulletin boards, as applicable.

paragraph.text: in subsection (g), as so redesignated, by striking 2024 and inserting 2027.
